text: Last summer, I planned a trip to Japan for two weeks. I spent weeks researching flights, hotels, and must-see places. Once there, I was amazed by the new culture—temples, shrines, and polite customs. The food was incredible, from fresh sushi to steaming ramen, though I struggled with language barriers. Simple tasks like ordering food or asking for directions became fun challenges. Despite the difficulties, the memories I made—sunset over Mount Fuji, a kind stranger helping me—are unforgettable. Travel taught me that even with careful planning, the best moments are often unplanned.
